target.title: [codex] Fail closed pair slash command routing
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves  Fixes #98239.  Running `/pair qr` from Control UI webchat could leave an externally managed Tailscale Serve Control UI route broken. The reported production route had Tailscale Serve proxying the HTTPS webchat URL to `http://127.0.0.1:18789` while OpenClaw later ended up with `gateway.bind=tailnet`, so the Gateway no longer served the loopback backend and the webchat URL returned HTTP 502.  The direct device-pair QR generator is non-mutating, but the reported production behavior showed an interrupted webchat task re-applying a `gateway.bind=tailnet` change after manual loopback restore. The likely writer is a missed plugin command falling through to a normal agent turn, especially with `/ pair qr` syntax or an unavailable command runtime. Once the agent turn owns the request, it can attempt configuration repair and restart the Gateway.  ## Why This Change Was Made  This PR keeps device pairing URL generation and slash dispatch from silently widening `gateway.bind` or falling into a general agent turn:  - accepts `/ pair qr` as the registered `/pair` plugin command, so whitespace after the slash does not bypass the plugin handler - reserves manifest-declared runtime slash commands when no runtime handler is registered, returning a deterministic plugin-unavailable response instead of continuing into the agent pipeline - filters manifest runtime slash reservations through plugin activation policy, so disabled or denied plugins do not reserve commands - keeps built-in/reserved aliases out of manifest-only reservations - gates manifest fallback responses through the normal command authorization check - updates loopback-only pairing guidance to keep `gateway.bind=loopback` for externally managed Tailscale Serve setups and use `gateway.tailscale.mode=serve`, `plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl`, or `gateway.remote.url` for the public mobile pairing URL - adds explicit model-facing gateway tool guard coverage for `gateway.bind` changes via `config.patch` and `config.apply`  ## User Impact  For users opening Control UI through Tailscale Serve, `/pair qr` should no longer be able to miss the plugin command and continue as a normal agent request that may mutate Gateway bind settings. If the device-pair runtime handler is not registered yet, users get a deterministic plugin-unavailable response instead of a hidden agent turn.  Externally managed Tailscale Serve setups also get clearer loopback guidance: keep the Gateway loopback bind stable and configure the public pairing URL through managed Serve mode, plugin `publicUrl`, or `gateway.remote.url`.  ## Evidence  Focused local validation:  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/command-control.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.test.ts src/plugins/commands.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.test.ts extensions/device-pair/index.test.ts src/pairing/setup-code.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm tsgo:core` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm check:import-cycles` - `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers` - `pnpm lint:auth:no-pairing-store-group` - `pnpm lint:auth:pairing-account-scope` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/command-detection.ts src/auto-reply/command-control.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.test.ts src/plugins/commands.ts src/plugins/commands.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/device-pair/index.test.ts extensions/device-pair/index.ts` - `git diff --check`  Review validation:  - `codex review --base origin/main` on head `434a7f18`: no actionable correctness issues found. - Local ClawSweeper review on head `434a7f18`: no code findings and no security findings; remaining blocker was real behavior proof, now added below.  Live after-fix behavior proof on the production VPS, with prod restored after proof:  - Backed up active config, Tailscale Serve status, and the installed global OpenClaw package. - Temporarily installed this PR package: `OpenClaw 2026.6.11 (434a7f1)`. - Recreated the reported precondition deliberately: `gateway.bind=loopback`; Tailscale Serve `/` -> `http://127.0.0.1:18789`; local health `200`; HTTPS Control UI root `200`; HTTPS `/healthz` `200`. - Used a temporary random gateway token for the proof window only; QR/setup contents and token were not printed in PR evidence. - Sent the Control UI/WebChat `chat.send` RPC for `/pair qr`: ACK `{"ok":true,"status":"started","runIdPresent":true}`. - Sent the Control UI/WebChat `chat.send` RPC for `/ pair qr`: ACK `{"ok":true,"status":"started","runIdPresent":true}`. - Waited 30 seconds after the sends. - After the wait: `gateway.bind=loopback`; Tailscale Serve `/` still -> `http://127.0.0.1:18789`; HTTPS Control UI root `200`; HTTPS `/healthz` `200`. - Restored production state: `OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (aa69b12)`; `gateway.bind=tailnet`; Tailscale Serve `/` -> `http://[redacted-tailnet-ip]:18789`; HTTPS Control UI root `200`; HTTPS `/healthz` `200`.  Redacted proof summary from VPS artifact `~/.openclaw/proof-98262-20260630T230842Z/proof-summary.txt`:  ```text before_version=OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (aa69b12) pr_version=OpenClaw 2026.6.11 (434a7f1) pre_bind=loopback pre_serve_root=http://127.0.0.1:18789 pair_qr_ack={"ok":true,"status":"started","runIdPresent":true} spaced_pair_qr_ack={"ok":true,"status":"started","runIdPresent":true} after_bind=loopback after_serve_root=http://127.0.0.1:18789 after_https_root=200 after_https_health=200 restore_bind=tailnet restore_serve_root=http://[redacted-tailnet-ip]:18789 restore_https_root=200 restore_https_health=200 ```  `pnpm check:changed` was attempted first. On this Windows host it delegated to Blacksmith Testbox and failed before running checks because the local Crabbox binary reports `main-a2c78e86` instead of a parseable semver. Running the local child wrapper also hung without output, so the relevant changed-check typecheck/lint/guard commands above were run directly.
target.author: brokemac79
